--- title: "Workers' health under climate change: Extreme events (Droughts)" description: Series of nine infographics describing the main risks to workers' health related to climate change and the environment [International Labour Organization (ILO) 2024](https://www.ilo.org/publications/ensuring-safety-and-health-work-changing-climate) date: 2026-02-09 date-modified: 2026-02-09 draft: false draft-mode: gone image: img/droughts_logo_02.png preview: false execute: echo: false categories: - Climate change - Workers Health - Tableau lang: en format: html: page-layout: full # code-tools: true embed-resources: true lightbox: true toc: false author: - name: Edgar Rodriguez-Huerta url: https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/research/beacons-of-excellence/rights-lab/our-team/edgar-rodríguez-huerta/index.aspx affiliation: University of Nottingham, Rights Lab affiliation-url: https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/research/beacons-of-excellence/rights-lab/index.aspx orcid: 0000-0002-6887-0040 --- ### Estimation Droughts • Droughts measured by 12-month Standardized Precipitation-Evapotranspiration Index (SPEI) [^1] Data visualization summarizes the analysis of excessive heat in **Brazil**, disaggregated by **municipality, population, and crops**, to identify spatial differences and heat stress hotspots across dimensions. By translating complex climate and socio-environmental data into accessible visual formats, data visualization supports better decision-making, enabling targeted interventions, risk prioritization, and policies that advance decent work in agriculture. --- ### Download {.appendix} {{< downloadthis img/01_viz_excessive_heat.pdf dname=clidewo_climate_hazard_excessive_heat label="Download the pdf" icon=file-earmark-pdf type=Warning >}} --- [^1]: Climate–Conflict–Vulnerability Index (CCVI). *Climate data (2024-Q4)*.
